texto: Whatever. There is a natural order to things, a hierarchy. And no less so in man. For man may be the master of nature, but he is also part of it. Every living thing, from the greatest of all men to the lowliest earthworm has its place. It is very important that the groundhog not think he is tiger, nor a sparrow believe he is a hawk. A frog would not make a very good shark, would it? One must know their place in the world" ~ Baroness von Berge, Greta Greaves of Austria
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Austin Scott Collins que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Whatever. There is a natural order to things, a hierarchy. And no less so in man. For man may be …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, personal-development, place-in-the-world, com foco em place-in-the-world e places.

texto: I do not run from beauty, my own or anyone else's. If it is a gift, I claim it and I use it. And if it is a curse, well...I claim it and use it." Victoria da Vinci
explicacao: Citação de Austin Scott Collins sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “I do not run from beauty, my own or anyone else's. If it is a gift, I claim it and I use it. And …” articula autoestima, beauty, confidence em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como confidence e self-awareness.
